latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 17:13
RNS Number : 0047V
Atlas Metals Group PLC
02 March 2026
2 March 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company
,
in conformity with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1R, announces that, as at 27 February 2026, its issued share capital comprised 30,532,930
ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
"). Each Ordinary Share carries the right to one vote in relation to all circumstances at general meetings of the Company. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury and therefore the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 30,532,930.
The above figure of 30,532,930 may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
- Ends -
For further information, please contact:
Atlas Metals Group plc:

latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 15:18
RNS Number : 7222T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
19 February 2026
19 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of equity
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company announces that, further to the Company's circular dated 5 November 2025 and the authorities approved by shareholders of the Company at the general meeting held on 1 December 2025, application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc (the "
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 1,408,000 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission relates to the allotment of 1,408,000 new Ordinary Shares ("
New Shares
") to be issued in terms of a historic settlement agreement.
Admission of the New Shares is expected to occur on 20 February 2026. The New Shares will rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 30,532,930 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 30,532,930. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Update on Proposed Acquisition
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0418T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
16 February 2026
16 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Proposed Acquisition
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company is pleased to provide an update
on the Company's proposed acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd ("
UPSA
") (the "
Proposed Acquisition
").
As detailed in the Company's announcement on 22 December 2025, the Company engaged SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd ("
SLR
"), to conduct a confirmatory drilling campaign, commencing on Lots 7 and 8 at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia, with the intention of proving a measured resource, and a technical memorandum has been provided by SLR. A total of 26 drillholes have now been completed using three drilling methods, diamond, percussion and blast hole, for a total of 1,265 metres.
From the samples collected during the drilling programme Quantitative X-Ray Diffraction (QXRD) and clay mineralogical analyses will be undertaken to characterise material properties relevant to assessing pozzolanic potential. Drill core pieces representing all lithological units were also selected for petrographic examination and X-ray Fluorescence (XRF) analysis to document mineralogical composition and textural features.
Initial volumes pending validation of drilling data, interpretation, geological modelling and material test results within Lots 7 and 8 indicate that the concept pit (see figure 1 below) covered by the areas of Lots 7 and 8 contains a total of
86.5 million tonnes of pozzolanic sandstone from a total volume of 151.75 million tonnes of material. Whilst these initial volumes are being assessed for resource status pending material testing and validation, the purpose of the update is to increase confidence in the volumes towards measured status. Volumes may vary based on further development of the geological model and validation.
Atlas Metals is expecting SLR to complete the updated Competent Person's Report ("
CPR
"), using data obtained from this drilling programme, which is expected to include certain JORC resources being upgraded from the inferred to measured category, prior to the completion of the Proposed Acquisition. The last published CPR on the Yammacoona Sand Quarry, as announced by the Company on 28 November 2025, may be found on the Company's website at:
https://atlasmetalsgroup.com/investors/reports
The Proposed Acquisition continues to progress in line with the Board's expectations and further announcements on the progress of the Proposed Acquisition will be made when appropriate.
Figure 1 - Concept Pit Design, showing the locations of blocks 7 and 8 (from SLR CPR 26 November 2025
Illustration: Recent SLR drilling on behalf of the Company at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG is a mining company focused on uranium exploration and development.  The firm holds a licence in Kyrgyzstan and is working to bring that asset into production, while also pursuing other mineral projects in the region.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is the only metal that powers nuclear reactors worldwide, making it a critical component of energy security and climate strategy.  AMG’s licence gives it a foothold in a high‑value, low‑competition market that is attracting new investment as governments push for clean energy.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the development phase – it has not yet produced any ore.  It has issued new equity to raise cash, and is negotiating a potential acquisition that could accelerate its path to production.  The stock is trading very low, reflecting the long‑term nature of the project and the need for additional capital.

rns_narrative: AMG has been busy filing updates that show a clear pivot toward securing the Kyrgyz uranium licence and preparing for a possible acquisition.  The latest filings detail new equity issues, a revised ATM facility, and a timetable for the acquisition process.

Two themes emerge: 1) Capital structure changes – the company is raising money and tightening its balance sheet.  2) Asset‑specific progress – the licence is the key to future cash flows, and the acquisition could bring immediate scale and expertise.
These moves suggest the management is trying to stabilize the company’s finances while positioning it for a big upside if the licence gets approved and the acquisition closes.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet interest from niche investors who see a long‑term play in uranium but are waiting for a catalyst.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders are optimistic that a successful licence approval or acquisition could unlock a large upside, especially if uranium prices rally.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many are concerned about regulatory delays, the high cost of development, and the fact that the company has yet to produce any revenue.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has been steady but thin; sentiment has not yet shifted dramatically.  The community is holding its breath for the next major announcement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for AMG is flat, meaning there is no mainstream hype.  A surprise catalyst would likely produce a sharp, unanticipated move.

near_term_catalysts: Regulatory approval of the Kyrgyz uranium licence – a single announcement could remove the biggest uncertainty.
Completion of the acquisition – if AMG can merge with a larger miner, it would gain access to proven infrastructure and a ready pipeline.
A spike in uranium spot prices – a 10‑15% lift could make the project immediately profitable.
A positive funding round that brings the company to a healthy cash balance.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a break above the 52‑week low, and any announcement that confirms licence approval or acquisition progress.  A 3‑day volume surge paired with a 5‑point price jump would be a clear buying signal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is in the early stages of a deep crash; the market has likely already capitulated.  The APEX score indicates a tight price range, so the next move will be a sharp breakout if the catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen well below its 52‑week range, creating a steep but symmetrical rebound opportunity.  If the price climbs to halfway between the current low and the 52‑week high, the upside could be 100‑200%.  Historically, deep crash signals (GREEN or RED) have produced 2‑3× gains once a catalyst triggers a bounce.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is regulatory delay or failure to secure the licence, which would leave the company without a viable asset.  A prolonged cash burn could also force a share dilution or even a sale at a low price, limiting upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 2‑3× versus a downside capped at the depth of the current crash (≈70‑80%).  The asymmetry is roughly 3:1 – you stand to make a lot more than you can lose if the catalyst hits.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 52‑week low or the nearest support level.  Size the position to risk no more than 5% of the portfolio on the entry.  Place a stop just below the support – a 2‑3% break would trigger an exit.  Hold through the first 3‑5 days of a breakout to capture momentum.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of at least 150% of average daily volume and a price move of 3‑5 points above the low.  A press release confirming licence approval or acquisition progress is the ideal trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or a regulatory body blocks the licence, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AMG sits at a deep crash point with a silent crowd and a potentially game‑changing licence or acquisition on the horizon; the upside is high and the downside is already capped, making a contrarian long a compelling play.
